_meta.source: Hand-curated from IMDA PSG pre-approval guide Qs + verified against data/raw scrapes (2026-05-19)

questions.t2_bullet: Comply with PDPA obligations and complete the PDP requirements form (go.gov.sg/pdp)
questions.question: Can your solution demonstrate compliance with the following Personal Data Protection requirements?

body_sections.heading: Compliance Requirements
body_sections.paragraph:
Digital solutions that collect, use, disclose, process or dispose
personal data should incorporate features that support the
obligations under the Personal Data Protection Act (2020).

To comply with this requirement, you MUST complete the Personal
Data Protection Requirements form at https://go.gov.sg/pdp.

questions.t2_bullet: Pass a third-party VA/PT within the last 12 months covering network, application, data, access, API, and cloud security (OWASP Top 10)
questions.question:
Has your solution undergone a comprehensive security vulnerability
assessment/penetration testing (VA/PT) conducted by a qualified
third-party within the last 12 months? The scope of the VA/PT must
cover network security; application security; data protection
measures and access control (if applicable); API security testing
(if applicable); Cloud security configuration review (if applicable).
Specifically, for web application security, the scope must cover
minimally all OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

body_sections.heading: Submission Requirements
body_sections.paragraph:
Please submit the VA/PT report (dated maximum 1 year from the
checklist submission date). The VA/PT Report must include:
bullets: Evidence of vulnerability fixes or mitigation plans

body_sections.paragraph:
If you are the reseller of the solution, please obtain the VA/PT
report from your product principal.

SOC 2 Type II report can be accepted if the detailed technical
vulnerability assessment results are part of the SOC2 Type II
scope.

body_sections.heading: Qualified Third-party Definition
body_sections.paragraph:
CREST-certified companies (https://www.crest-approved.org/members/)
or companies with security professionals holding relevant CREST
certifications; security professionals with recognised
certifications such as Offensive Security Certified Professional
(OSCP), EC-Council Certified Penetration Testing Professional
(CPENT), GIAC Penetration Tester (GPEN), or other equivalent
industry-recognised certifications.

inputs.label: Assessor / firm name + CREST (or equivalent) certification
inputs.placeholder: e.g. Acme Security Pte Ltd, CREST-certified

inputs.label: Brief scope summary
inputs.placeholder: e.g. Network + application + API + cloud config; OWASP Top 10 covered

questions.t2_bullet: Declare whether you are the Product Principal or a Reseller for this solution
questions.question: Are you the Product Principal of the solution that you are submitting for pre-approval?

questions.t2_bullet: Hold CSA Cyber Essentials for ICT Vendor (or Cyber Trust Mark / ISO27001) for the Product Principal entity
questions.question:
Has your organisation achieved CSA Cyber Essentials for ICT Vendor
Mark certification or equivalent recognised cybersecurity
certifications (including but not limited to Cyber Trust Mark or
ISO27001) that validate the implementation of appropriate security
controls against common cyber threats in your organisation and the
solution you are submitting for pre-approval?

body_sections.heading: Compliance Note
body_sections.paragraph:
Vendors are encouraged to comply at application and are required
to meet this requirement by the Annual Review, where it will be
assessed as mandatory.

body_sections.heading: Reference
body_sections.paragraph:
For more information on the Cyber Essentials Mark, refer to
https://www.csa.gov.sg/cyber-essentials/.

questions.t2_bullet: Submit certificate, scope, and issuance date for the Cyber Essentials / equivalent attestation
questions.question: Please specify the following information.

question_extras.heading: Certification Requirements
bullets: The certificate demonstrating your organisation has attained Cyber Essentials for ICT Vendors
The cybersecurity certification the organisation has met

body_sections.paragraph:
Please also upload a copy of the Certification and indicate the
Certification Issuance Date in the date field. (Final upload
happens at PSG submission, not in this survey.)

inputs.label: Description / Details
inputs.placeholder: e.g. ISO 27001:2022 covering cloud-hosted ERP services for Customer Master, Sales, Finance modules

questions.t2_bullet: Hold CSA Cyber Essentials Mark (or Cyber Trust Mark / ISO27001) for the reseller entity
questions.question:
Has your organisation achieved CSA Cyber Essentials Mark certification
or equivalent recognised cybersecurity certifications (including but
not limited to Cyber Trust Mark or ISO27001) that validate the
implementation of appropriate security controls against common cyber
threats in your organisation and the solution you are submitting for
pre-approval?

body_sections.heading: Compliance Note
body_sections.paragraph:
Vendors are encouraged to comply at application and are required
to meet this requirement by the Annual Review, where it will be
assessed as mandatory.

body_sections.heading: Reference
body_sections.paragraph:
For more information on the Cyber Essentials Mark, refer to
https://www.csa.gov.sg/cyber-essentials/.

questions.t2_bullet: Submit certificate, scope, and issuance date for the reseller's cybersecurity attestation
questions.question: Please specify the following information.

question_extras.heading: Certification Requirements
bullets: The cybersecurity certification the organisation has met

body_sections.paragraph:
Please also upload a copy of the Certification and indicate the
Certification Issuance Date in the date field. (Final upload
happens at PSG submission, not in this survey.)

inputs.label: Description / Details
inputs.placeholder: e.g. ISO 27001:2022 covering hosted ERP platform for all modules in scope
